name: Inventory Carrying Cost
description: The annual cost of holding inventory: the carrying-cost rate applied to the average value of inventory held.

inputs.name: Carrying cost rate
inputs.description: Annual carrying cost as a fraction of inventory value, e.g. 0.26 for 26%.

assumptions: The carrying-cost rate captures capital, storage, service, and risk costs.
Average inventory value is representative of the year.
limitations: The rate is policy-dependent; components vary by firm (typical range ~15-30%).
Derive the rate as annual carrying costs / average inventory value when a firm-specific figure is needed.

citation.quote: if the average inventory value of a company is US$2 million, the carrying cost is 2,000,000 x 26% or US$520,000.

citations.quote: The carrying cost is calculated by multiplying the carrying cost percentage by the average value of the items being stored.
If the average value of the inventory is $12 million, the percentage of carrying cost for the warehouse is 20.3% (2,428,000/12,000,000).
